raw_excerpt_key_facts: Verbatim from the live JD Supra / Orrick article (fetched 2026-06-05 ~10:25 CT for cross-check): [Title] 'FDA Announces Removal of 12 Peptides from Category 2 and Schedules PCAC Meetings to Consider Adding Peptides to 503A Bulk Drug Substances List'. [Date] 'April 17, 2026'. [Authors] 'Shari Esfahani, Thora Johnson, Amy Joseph, Georgia Ravitz, Jeremy Sherer (Orrick, Herrington & Sutcliffe LLP)'. [Key fact verbatim] 'On April 15, 2026, the U.S. Food and Drug Administration ("FDA") announced a major shift in the regulatory landscape for compounded peptides. FDA updated its Bulk Drug Substances Nominated for Use in Compounding Under Section 503A list, providing notice that after 7 days, it will remove 12 peptide bulk drug substances from Category 2 of its Section 503A bulk drug substances list — the category reserved for substances the agency has determined raise significant safety concerns — due to their nominations being withdrawn by the nominators.' [Second meeting verbatim] 'FDA simultaneously published a Federal Register Notice announcing it will convene the Pharmacy Compounding Advisory Committee ("PCAC") at public meetings on July 23–24, 2026, and before the end of February 2027 to discuss whether these peptides should be added to the 503A bulks list'. [GHK-Cu verbatim] 'Additionally, the FDA is removing one peptide, GHK-Cu, from Category 1 (substances under evaluation that have not been identified as presenting significant safety risks and as to which FDA is exercising enforcement discretion) for the same reason — withdrawal of the nomination — with PCAC consultation planned before the end of February 2027.' [Kennedy Rogan verbatim] 'On February 27, 2026, HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. appeared on The Joe Rogan Experience and announced that the FDA was expected to take action imminently regarding peptides that had been restricted under the Biden administration.' [Kennedy 19-vs-14 verbatim] 'asserted that the agency under the Biden administration had "illegally" reclassified 19 peptides into Category 2 in September 2023.' (vs Kennedy's Rogan statement of 'approximately 14 peptides' more accessible.) [Effective date verbatim] 'Once these peptides are formally removed from Category 2 (approximately by April 22, 2026)'. [Compounding guidance verbatim] 'Stakeholders should closely monitor this process but should not begin compounding with any of the 12 peptides solely on the basis of their removal from Category 2. Until the FDA affirmatively places these substances on the 503A bulks list or into Category 1 with enforcement discretion, compounding these substances carries regulatory risk.' [PCAC precedent verbatim] 'There is prior precedent from the Biden administration when several peptides previously on the Category 2 list were similarly withdrawn by nominators and removed from the list and were then brought before the PCAC, which recommended against inclusion on the 503 bulks list in each case. However, the current environment is materially different: many prior FDA staff have departed, the PCAC has significant vacancies that Secretary Kennedy could fill before the July meeting, and the administration has publicly signaled support for broader peptide access.' [2024 reference verbatim] 'public advisory committee meetings convened in 2024 resulted in the PCAC voting against inclusion of all previous peptides under review'.
diff_against_researcher_capture: No substantive differences. Researcher correctly captured: (a) date (April 17, 2026), (b) authors, (c) full 12-peptide Category 2 removal + 7-day timeline, (d) GHK-Cu non-injectable Category 1 removal + second PCAC meeting before end of February 2027, (e) Kennedy Rogan '14 peptides' framing, (f) 19-vs-12-vs-14 number-family distinction (Biden reclassified 19, Kennedy said ~14, FDA action covers 12), (g) 'approximately by April 22, 2026' effective date, (h) 'compounding carries regulatory risk' framing, (i) 2024 PCAC precedent. Verifier independently confirmed all of these from the live Orrick page.
